title: Existence of interior-supported primitives $\alpha_i$ (compactly-supported de Rham; integral vs. real vanishing)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The definition of the linking invariant $L_h(g)=\int_z\alpha_1\wedge g^*\omega_2$ requires forms $\alpha_i$ that are (i) supported in $\mathrm{Int}(M)$ and (ii) satisfy $d\alpha_i=g^*\omega_i$. The file justifies their existence with one sentence:

> "By the de Rham theorem for cohomology with compact support, it follows that there are piecewise smooth differential forms $\alpha_1,\alpha_2$..."

**What needs to be proved/clarified:**
1. State the compactly-supported de Rham isomorphism $H^k_c(\mathrm{Int}\,M;\RR)\cong H^k(M,\partial M;\RR)$ precisely.
2. The hypothesis is $g^*\Omega_i=0\in H^2(M,\partial M;\ZZ)$ (**integral** coefficients). One must pass to real coefficients: the integral class vanishing implies the **real** class vanishes (torsion is killed by $\otimes\RR$), hence $g^*\omega_i$ is exact via an interior-supported primitive. This integral-vs-real step is exactly what the one-line citation hides and should be written out.
3. Piecewise-smooth regularity of $\alpha_i$ should be noted.

This is an unjustified key step (F3) underpinning the entire linking-invariant machinery.
